usage: PROG process-dir [-h] [--data-root DATA_ROOT] [--repo-id REPO_ID]
 [--user-agent USER_AGENT] [--languages LANGUAGES]
 [--all-languages] [--no-full-text]
 [--max-articles-per-qid MAX_ARTICLES_PER_QID]
 [--enrichment-batch-size ENRICHMENT_BATCH_SIZE]
 [--enrichment-site-workers ENRICHMENT_SITE_WORKERS]
 [--limit LIMIT] [--skip-existing] [--force] [--push]
 [--commit-message COMMIT_MESSAGE]
 [--upload-threads UPLOAD_THREADS] [--hf-token HF_TOKEN]
 [--log-level {DEBUG,INFO,WARNING,ERROR}] [--dry-run]
 input

positional arguments:
 input Directory containing *.osm.pbf files

options:
 -h, --help show this help message and exit
 --data-root DATA_ROOT Data root directory
 --repo-id REPO_ID Hugging Face repo id (org/name)
 --user-agent USER_AGENT Wikipedia/Wikidata UA
 --languages LANGUAGES Optional comma-separated language codes
 --all-languages Fetch all available sitelinks
 --no-full-text Skip Wikipedia full-text fetch
 --max-articles-per-qid MAX_ARTICLES_PER_QID
 --enrichment-batch-size ENRICHMENT_BATCH_SIZE
 --enrichment-site-workers ENRICHMENT_SITE_WORKERS
 --limit LIMIT Cap number of polygons per PBF
 --skip-existing
 --force
 --push Push artifacts to Hugging Face
 --commit-message COMMIT_MESSAGE
 --upload-threads UPLOAD_THREADS
 Concurrent Hugging Face upload workers per atomic commit
 --hf-token HF_TOKEN Hugging Face write token. Defaults to the HF_TOKEN env var or the
 saved `huggingface-cli login` token.
 --log-level {DEBUG,INFO,WARNING,ERROR}
 --dry-run Use a stub HF client (no network)
